summary: Shebang, strict shell options and script identity metadata; canonical error code constants and aliasing.
raw_snippet:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# =============================================================================
# GroqBash⁺ — Bash-first wrapper for the Groq API
# File: groqbash
# Version: 2.0.0
# Copyright (C) 2026 Cristian Evangelisti
# License: GPL-3.0-or-later
# =============================================================================
# Requirements (no fallbacks): bash coreutils findutils util-linux gawk curl jq

########################################
# SECTION: PRECORE_BOOT - BEGIN
########################################
#--1<---[ SECTION: PRECORE_BOOT_SETUP_SHELL ]--->1--
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_NAME="groqbash"
SCRIPT_VERSION="2.0.0"
SCRIPT_DATE="2026-05-07"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Canonical error codes
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROQBASH_ERR_NO_API_KEY=10
GROQBASH_ERR_BAD_MODEL=11
GROQBASH_ERR_CURL_FAILED=12
GROQBASH_ERR_INVALID_JSON=13
GROQBASH_ERR_NO_PROMPT=14
GROQBASH_ERR_TMP=15
GROQBASH_ERR_API=16

GROQBASHERRNOAPIKEY=$GROQBASH_ERR_NO_API_KEY
GROQBASHERRBAD_MODEL=$GROQBASH_ERR_BAD_MODEL
GROQBASHERRCURL_FAILED=$GROQBASH_ERR_CURL_FAILED
GROQBASHERRINVALID_JSON=$GROQBASH_ERR_INVALID_JSON
GROQBASHERRNO_PROMPT=$GROQBASH_ERR_NO_PROMPT
GROQBASHERRTMP=$GROQBASH_ERR_TMP
GROQBASHERRAPI=$GROQBASH_ERR_API

summary: Dev/test guard to allow sourcing the script without running main flow; returns early when GROQBASH_SOURCE_ONLY is set.
raw_snippet:
#--2<---[ SECTION: PRECORE_BOOT_SETUP_ENV_CMDS ]--->2--
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Dev/Test guard for sourcing only
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# When set to 1, importing the script (GROQBASH_SOURCE_ONLY=1 . ./groqbash)
# will load function definitions without executing the main runtime flow.
if [ "${GROQBASH_SOURCE_ONLY:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
  # If sourced from a shell, return to the caller; if executed directly,
  # exit with success to avoid running the main program.
  return 0 2>/dev/null || exit 0
fi

summary: Verify presence of mandatory external commands; abort with error if any required command is missing.
raw_snippet:
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Verify mandatory commands (no fallback)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
for cmd in bash jq curl mktemp stat flock base64 find awk sed grep xargs tr sort head wc tee date mv chmod cp rm printf; do
  if ! command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    printf 'groqbash: ERROR: required command not found: %s\n' "$cmd" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
done

summary: Resolve script directory and set GROQBASH_DIR defaulting logic; determine extras and providers directories and export them.
raw_snippet:
#--4<---[ SECTION: PRECORE_BOOT_DIR_PATH ]--->4--
SCRIPTDIR="$(resolve_script_dir)"
# Allow external override of repository root via GROQBASH_ROOT.
# Priority: explicit GROQBASH_DIR env > GROQBASH_ROOT env > SCRIPTDIR-derived default.
if [ -n "${GROQBASH_DIR:-}" ]; then
  : # keep explicit GROQBASH_DIR from environment
elif [ -n "${GROQBASH_ROOT:-}" ]; then
  GROQBASH_DIR="${GROQBASH_ROOT%/}/groqbash.d"
else
  GROQBASH_DIR="$SCRIPTDIR/groqbash.d"
fi

# Canonical runtime destination for extras (always under groqbash.d)
CANONICAL_EXTRAS_DIR="${GROQBASH_DIR%/}/extras"
LEGACY_EXTRAS_DIR="${SCRIPTDIR%/}/extras"

# Export canonical extras and providers dirs
GROQBASH_EXTRAS_DIR="${CANONICAL_EXTRAS_DIR}"
# Providers shipped as extras live under GROQBASH_EXTRAS_DIR by default;
# fall back to a providers dir under canonical config dir only if extras not set.
PROVIDERS_DIR="${PROVIDERS_DIR:-${GROQBASH_EXTRAS_DIR%/}/providers}"
# Ensure a canonical fallback exists if extras not configured
: "${PROVIDERS_DIR:=$(canonical_config_dir)/providers}"
export GROQBASH_EXTRAS_DIR PROVIDERS_DIR

summary: Define standard directories (config, models, templates, history, tmp) and model file defaults; enforce invariants for GROQBASH_DIR and GROQBASH_TMPDIR.
raw_snippet:
# Standard directories (derived from GROQBASH_DIR, overridable via env)
GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR="${GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR:-$GROQBASH_DIR/config}"
GROQBASH_MODELS_DIR="${GROQBASH_MODELS_DIR:-$GROQBASH_DIR/models}"
GROQBASH_TEMPLATES_DIR="${GROQBASH_TEMPLATES_DIR:-$GROQBASH_DIR/templates}"
GROQBASH_HISTORY_DIR="${GROQBASH_HISTORY_DIR:-$GROQBASH_DIR/history}"
GROQBASH_TMPDIR="${GROQBASH_TMPDIR:-$GROQBASH_DIR/tmp}"
MODELS_FILE="${MODELS_FILE:-$GROQBASH_MODELS_DIR/models.txt}"
MAX_MODELS="${MAX_MODELS:-200}"

# Validate GROQBASH_DIR / GROQBASH_TMPDIR invariants
if [ -z "${GROQBASH_DIR:-}" ]; then
  log_error "TMP" "GROQBASH_DIR is empty; cannot derive GROQBASH_TMPDIR"
  exit "${GROQBASHERRTMP:-1}"
fi

# Forbid using /tmp or subpaths of /tmp as GROQBASH_TMPDIR
case "${GROQBASH_TMPDIR:-}" in
  /tmp|/tmp/*)
    log_error "TMP" "GROQBASH_TMPDIR must not be /tmp or under /tmp: ${GROQBASH_TMPDIR}"
    exit "${GROQBASHERRTMP:-1}"
    ;;
esac

# Enforce GROQBASH_TMPDIR inside GROQBASH_DIR perimeter
case "${GROQBASH_TMPDIR:-}" in
  "${GROQBASH_DIR}"|"${GROQBASH_DIR}"/*)
    : # ok
    ;;
  *)
    log_error "TMP" "GROQBASH_TMPDIR must be inside GROQBASH_DIR: ${GROQBASH_TMPDIR} (root: ${GROQBASH_DIR})"
    exit "${GROQBASHERRTMP:-1}"
    ;;
esac

# Defensive normalization: ensure GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR is non-empty and normalized
GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR="${GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR%/}"
if [ -z "$GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR" ]; then
  GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR="${GROQBASH_DIR%/}/config"
fi

summary: Ensure configuration directory exists and is writable by creating it and performing a quick writability check via a temp file.
raw_snippet:
# Helper: ensure config dir exists and is usable before any writes
ensure_config_dir() {
  # Normalize
  GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR="${GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR%/}"
  if [ -z "$GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR" ]; then
    GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR="${GROQBASH_DIR%/}/config"
  fi

  # Try to create directory (idempotent)
  if ! mkdir -p "${GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR}" 2>/dev/null; then
    log_error "CONFIG" "cannot create config dir: ${GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR}"
    return 1
  fi

  # Enforce strict perms
  chmod 700 "${GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR}" 2>/dev/null || true

  # Quick writability check: try to create a temp file inside
  if ! : > "${GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR%/}/.groqbash_tmp_check" 2>/dev/null; then
    log_error "CONFIG" "config dir not writable: ${GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR}"
    return 1
  else
    rm -f "${GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR%/}/.groqbash_tmp_check" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi

  return 0
}

# Ensure config dir exists now (fail early if not)
ensure_config_dir || { log_error "CONFIG" "config dir unavailable; aborting."; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }

summary: Write provider-url helper definition (function) and resolve_provider_url function defined later; then fail-fast if canonical_config_dir is empty.
raw_snippet:
# Write provider-url file (single-line, non-secret). Best-effort; do not write API keys.
# Usage: write_provider_url_if_missing <provider> <url>
write_provider_url_if_missing() {
  local prov="$1" url="$2" file dir tmp
  [ -z "$prov" ] && return 1
  [ -z "$url" ] && return 1
  file="$(canonical_provider_url_file)"
  dir="$(dirname "$file")"
  mkdir -p "$dir" 2>/dev/null || return 1
  # If file already exists and non-empty, do nothing
  if [ -f "$file" ] && [ -s "$file" ]; then
    return 0
  fi
  # Write atomically into RUN_TMPDIR if available, else directly (best-effort)
  if [ -n "${RUN_TMPDIR:-}" ] && [ -d "${RUN_TMPDIR:-}" ]; then
    tmp="$(mktemp "${RUN_TMPDIR%/}/provider-url.XXXX" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  else
    tmp="$(mktemp 2>/dev/null || true)"
  fi
  if [ -n "$tmp" ]; then
    printf '%s\n' "$url" > "$tmp"
    mv -f "$tmp" "$file" 2>/dev/null || cp -f "$tmp" "$file" 2>/dev/null || { rm -f "$tmp" 2>/dev/null || true; return 1; }
    chmod 600 "$file" 2>/dev/null || true
    return 0
  else
    # fallback: write directly
    printf '%s\n' "$url" > "$file" 2>/dev/null || return 1
    chmod 600 "$file" 2>/dev/null || true
    return 0
  fi
}

# Resolve provider URL with priority: ENV (GROQBASH_API_URL / GROQBASH_PROVIDER_URL) > provider-url file > embedded default (groq)
# Usage: resolve_provider_url <provider>
resolve_provider_url() {
  local prov="${1:-$PROVIDER}" prov_file prov_val
  # 1) ENV
  if [ -n "${GROQBASH_API_URL:-}" ]; then
    GROQBASH_PROVIDER_URL="${GROQBASH_API_URL}"
    export GROQBASH_PROVIDER_URL
    return 0
  fi
  if [ -n "${GROQBASH_PROVIDER_URL:-}" ]; then
    return 0
  fi
  # 2) provider-url file
  prov_file="$(canonical_provider_url_file)"
  if [ -f "$prov_file" ] && [ -s "$prov_file" ]; then
    prov_val="$(sed -n '1p' "$prov_file" 2>/dev/null | awk '{$1=$1;print}')"
    if [ -n "$prov_val" ]; then
      GROQBASH_PROVIDER_URL="$prov_val"
      export GROQBASH_PROVIDER_URL
      return 0
    fi
  fi
  # 3) embedded default for groq only (minimal)
  if [ "${prov:-}" = "groq" ]; then
    GROQBASH_PROVIDER_URL="https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/chat/completions"
    export GROQBASH_PROVIDER_URL
    return 0
  fi
  return 1
}

# Fail-fast if canonical_config_dir is empty for any reason
if [ -z "$(canonical_config_dir)" ]; then
  printf 'groqbash: ERROR: CONFIG: canonical config dir is empty; aborting.\n' >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Canonical PROVIDER_FILE path built only from GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR
PROVIDER_FILE="$(canonical_provider_file)"

summary: Early CLI helpers: handle immediate print flags (--print-config-dir, --print-provider-file, --print-model-file) and exit early when matched.
raw_snippet:
#--6<---[ SECTION: PRECORE_BOOT_CLI_HELPERS ]--->6--
# Internal CLI helpers: print canonical config paths (safe, single-file)
# These flags are handled early and exit immediately after printing.
# Supported forms:
#   ./groqbash --print-config-dir
#   ./groqbash --print-provider-file
#   ./groqbash --print-model-file <provider>
#   ./groqbash --print-model-file=<provider>
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; then
  # Iterate through args to support flags anywhere on the command line
  i=1
  while [ "$i" -le "$#" ]; do
    arg="${!i}"
    case "$arg" in
      --print-config-dir)
        printf '%s\n' "$(canonical_config_dir)"
        exit 0
        ;;
      --print-provider-file)
        printf '%s\n' "$(canonical_provider_file)"
        exit 0
        ;;
      --print-model-file)
        next_index=$((i+1))
        if [ "$next_index" -le "$#" ]; then
          prov="${!next_index}"
          printf '%s\n' "$(canonical_model_file "$prov")"
          exit 0
        else
          printf 'groqbash: ERROR: usage: --print-model-file <provider>\n' >&2
          exit 2
        fi
        ;;        
      --print-model-file=*)
        prov="${arg#--print-model-file=}"
        printf '%s\n' "$(canonical_model_file "$prov")"
        exit 0
        ;;
      # allow short-circuit if user passed combined flags like --help etc.
      --help|-h)
        # fall through to normal help handling later
        break
        ;;
      *)
        # not one of our print flags; continue scanning
        ;;
    esac
    i=$((i+1))
  done
fi

summary: Session MVP helpers are defined as functions; alla fine è presente un default per LAST_CHECK_LINES (assegnazione top-level).
raw_snippet:
#--4<---[ SECTION: PRECORE_RUN_SESSION_MVP ]--->4--
# Session helpers (MVP - Minimum Viable Product)
# - session_validate_id <id> -> 0 if valid, 1 if invalid
# - session_now_ts -> prints UTC timestamp YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ
# - session_messages_tmp_path <session_id> -> prints path under RUN_TMPDIR
# - session_read_window <session_id> <N> <out_file> -> reads last N NDJSON lines, normalizes roles, writes {"messages":[...]}
# - session_append <session_id> <role> <content> <meta_json> -> idempotent append under lock
# - session_sanitize_cmd <cmd> -> prints sanitized cmd (truncate, remove env-like KEY=VAL, redact tokens)
# - session_marker_create <message_id> -> create marker file in RUN_TMPDIR to avoid double append in same process
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

session_validate_id() {
  local id="$1"
  if [ -z "$id" ]; then return 1; fi
  if printf '%s' "$id" | grep -qE '^[A-Za-z0-9._-]{1,128}$'; then return 0; else return 1; fi
}

session_now_ts() {
  # UTC timestamp, seconds resolution, format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ
  date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ
}

session_messages_tmp_path() {
  local sid="$1"
  ensure_run_tmpdir || return 1
  printf '%s' "$RUN_TMPDIR/session-${sid}-messages.json"
}

session_sanitize_cmd() {
  local cmd="$1"
  # Remove env-like KEY=VAL, redact tokens/keys, truncate to 256 chars
  local sanitized
  sanitized="$(printf '%s' "$cmd" | sed -E 's/[A-Za-z0-9_]+=([^[:space:]]+)//g' | sed -E 's/(token|key|secret)[^[:space:]]*/[REDACTED]/Ig' )"
  printf '%s' "$(printf '%s' "$sanitized" | cut -c1-256)"
}

summary: Top-level CLI variable initializations followed by the main CLI parsing while-loop that consumes argv and sets flags/variables.
Session Engine loader: top-level logic that conditionally sources an optional session engine module and sets SE_AVAILABLE with validation of required functions.
raw_snippet:
SE_ENGINE_PATH="${GROQBASH_EXTRAS_DIR%/}/session/session-engine.sh"
SE_AVAILABLE=0
if [ -f "$SE_ENGINE_PATH" ] && [ "${GROQBASH_SESSION_ENGINE:-on}" != "off" ]; then
  # Source safely (no subshell) so functions become visible in current shell
  # If sourcing fails (syntax error), abort loading and keep SE_AVAILABLE=0
  if bash -n "$SE_ENGINE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then
    . "$SE_ENGINE_PATH"
    # Validate minimal interface
    if type session_engine_build_window >/dev/null 2>&1 && type session_engine_append >/dev/null 2>&1 && type session_engine_enabled >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      if session_engine_enabled 2>/dev/null; then
        SE_AVAILABLE=1
        if [ "${DEBUG:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
          log_info "SESSION" "Session Engine loaded and available"
        fi
      else
        SE_AVAILABLE=0
        if [ "${DEBUG:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
          log_info "SESSION" "Session Engine loaded but disabled by module"
        fi
      fi
    else
      SE_AVAILABLE=0
      log_warn "SESSION" "Session Engine module missing required functions; falling back to legacy"
    fi
  else
    SE_AVAILABLE=0
    log_warn "SESSION" "Session Engine module has syntax errors; falling back to legacy"
  fi
else
  # No engine file present or explicitly disabled
  SE_AVAILABLE=0
  if [ -n "${SESSION_ID:-}" ]; then
    if [ "${DEBUG:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
      log_info "SESSION" "Session Engine not available; using legacy session helpers"
    fi
  fi
fi

# Usage note: callers should attempt session_engine_* only when SE_AVAILABLE=1,
# and otherwise call legacy session_read_window/session_append as fallback.

  extras_prov_dir="$(canonical_config_dir)/providers"
fi
if [ -d "$extras_prov_dir" ]; then
  for pf in "$extras_prov_dir"/*.sh; do
    [ -e "$pf" ] || continue
    base="$(basename "$pf" .sh)"
    # skip duplicate embedded name
    [ "$base" = "groq" ] && continue
    _supported_providers_arr+=("$base")
  done
fi
SUPPORTED_PROVIDERS="$(printf '%s ' "${_supported_providers_arr[@]}" | sed 's/ $//')"

# Non-interactive machine-readable provider listing
if [ "${LIST_PROVIDERS_RAW:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
  # If no providers found or providers dir missing -> exit 1
  if [ "${#_supported_providers_arr[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
    exit 1
  fi
  # Print one provider name per line, no decoration, no sourcing
  for p in "${_supported_providers_arr[@]}"; do
    printf '%s\n' "$p"
  done
  exit 0
fi

# Non-interactive machine-readable models listing (one normalized model name per line)
if [ "${LIST_MODELS_RAW:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
  # If CLI provided a provider override, honor it as context but do NOT source provider modules
  if [ -n "${PROVIDER_CLI:-}" ]; then
    PROVIDER="${PROVIDER_CLI}"
  fi

  # MODELS_FILE is the canonical source of truth
  if [ -f "${MODELS_FILE:-}" ] && [ -s "${MODELS_FILE:-}" ]; then
    # Normalize entries: strip leading "models/" and common provider prefixes, trim whitespace
    # Keep only non-empty lines; print one model per line, no annotations
    awk '{
      g=$0
      sub(/^models\//,"",g)
      sub(/^groq[:\/ -]*/,"",g)
      sub(/^[[:space:]]+/,"",g)
      sub(/[[:space:]]+$/,"",g)
      if (g != "") print g
    }' "$MODELS_FILE"
    exit 0
  else
    # File missing or empty
    exit 2
  fi
fi

summary: Immediate actions handling: LIST_PROVIDERS, LIST_MODELS, SET_DEFAULT_MODEL, STREAM_MODE compatibility check and early exits; these are top-level conditional action blocks executed after CLI parsing.
raw_snippet:
if [ "$LIST_PROVIDERS" -eq 1 ]; then
  printf 'Available providers:\n' >&2
  printf '  - groq (embedded)\n' >&2
  extras_prov_dir="$PROVIDERS_DIR"
  if [ -d "$extras_prov_dir" ]; then
    for f in "$extras_prov_dir"/*.sh; do
      [ -e "$f" ] || continue

      printf '  - %s\n' "$base" >&2
    done

fi

if [ "$LIST_MODELS" -eq 1 ]; then

fi

if [ -n "$SET_DEFAULT_MODEL" ]; then
  # Ensure canonical config dir exists and is safe
  if ! mkdir -p "$(canonical_config_dir)" 2>/dev/null; then
    log_error "SEC" "cannot create config dir: $(canonical_config_dir)"; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
  fi
  if [ -L "$(canonical_config_dir)" ]; then log_error "SEC" "config dir is a symlink."; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; fi

  # Determine the active provider robustly:
  if [ -n "${PROVIDER_CLI:-}" ]; then
    target_provider="${PROVIDER_CLI}"
  elif [ -f "$(canonical_provider_file)" ] && [ -s "$(canonical_provider_file)" ]; then
    target_provider="$(sed -n '1p' "$(canonical_provider_file)" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    target_provider="$(printf '%s' "$target_provider" | awk '{$1=$1;print}')"
    [ -z "$target_provider" ] && target_provider="${PROVIDER:-groq}"
  else
    target_provider="${PROVIDER:-groq}"
  fi

  # Persist default model for the explicit/active provider under canonical config dir
  target_model_file="$(canonical_model_file "${target_provider}")"
  printf '%s\n' "$SET_DEFAULT_MODEL" | atomic_write "$target_model_file" 10 \
    || { log_error "SEC" "cannot write default model for provider ${target_provider} to ${target_model_file}."; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }
  chmod 600 "$target_model_file" 2>/dev/null || true

  printf 'groqbash: INFO: Default model for provider %s set to: %s\n' "$target_provider" "$SET_DEFAULT_MODEL" >&2
  exit 0
fi

if [ "$STREAM_MODE" -eq 1 ] && { [ "$OUTPUT_MODE" = "json" ] || [ "$OUTPUT_MODE" = "pretty" ]; }; then
  log_error "ARGS" "--stream incompatible with --json/--pretty."

fi

summary: Install-extras flow: large top-level block that validates source/destination, prevents nested copies, collects files, validates ownership and types, copies files atomically, reports installed components and performs syntax checks; executed when --install-extras was requested.
raw_snippet:
if [ "${INSTALL_EXTRAS:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
  # Determine chosen source: explicit override wins, otherwise default to SCRIPTDIR/extras
  if [ -n "${INSTALL_EXTRAS_SRC:-}" ]; then
    SRC_BASE="${INSTALL_EXTRAS_SRC%/}"
  else
    SRC_BASE="${SCRIPTDIR%/}/extras"
  fi

  # Canonicalize chosen source and destination (use cd && pwd for portability)
  if [ -d "${SRC_BASE}" ]; then
    SRC_BASE="$(cd "${SRC_BASE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 && pwd || printf '%s' "${SRC_BASE}")"
  else
    SRC_BASE="${SRC_BASE%/}"
  fi
  DEST_BASE="$(cd "${GROQBASH_EXTRAS_DIR%/}" >/dev/null 2>&1 && pwd || printf '%s' "${GROQBASH_EXTRAS_DIR%/}")"
  # Destination for provider assets: prefer explicit PROVIDERS_DIR, otherwise use DEST_BASE/providers.
  DEST_PROV="${PROVIDERS_DIR:-${DEST_BASE:-$(canonical_config_dir)}/providers}"

  # Legacy detection: only fatal if a legacy dir exists and is neither the canonical destination
  # nor the chosen source. Do not treat SCRIPTDIR/extras as fatal when it is the chosen source.
  if [ -d "${LEGACY_EXTRAS_DIR}" ]; then
    LEGACY_REAL="$(cd "${LEGACY_EXTRAS_DIR}" >/dev/null 2>&1 && pwd || printf '%s' "${LEGACY_EXTRAS_DIR}")"
    if [ "${LEGACY_REAL}" != "${DEST_BASE}" ] && [ "${LEGACY_REAL}" != "${SRC_BASE}" ]; then
      printf 'groqbash: ERROR: legacy extras directory found at %s\n' "${LEGACY_REAL}" >&2
      printf 'groqbash: ERROR: only %s is supported as runtime destination. Move or remove the legacy directory and retry.\n' "${DEST_BASE}" >&2
      exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
    fi
  fi

  # Validate source existence and type
  if [ ! -d "${SRC_BASE}" ]; then
    log_error "EXTRAS" "missing extras source directory: ${SRC_BASE}"
    exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
  fi
  if [ -L "${SRC_BASE}" ]; then
    log_error "EXTRAS" "extras source is a symlink: ${SRC_BASE}"
    exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
  fi

  # Prevent copying when source equals destination or source is inside destination (avoid nested copies)
  if [ "${SRC_BASE}" = "${DEST_BASE}" ]; then
    log_error "EXTRAS" "extras source and destination are identical (${SRC_BASE}); nothing to do."
    exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
  fi
  case "${SRC_BASE}/" in
    "${DEST_BASE}/"*) log_error "EXTRAS" "extras source ${SRC_BASE} is inside destination ${DEST_BASE}; aborting to avoid nested copy."; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP" ;;
  esac

  # Prepare destination directories with strict perms
  mkdir -p "${DEST_BASE}" "${DEST_PROV}" 2>/dev/null || { log_error "EXTRAS" "cannot create extras dest: ${DEST_BASE}"; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }
  chmod 700 "${DEST_BASE}" 2>/dev/null || true

  # Gather regular files from source
  files_to_copy=()
  while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do files_to_copy+=("$f"); done < <(find "${SRC_BASE}" -type f -print0 2>/dev/null || true)

  if [ "${#files_to_copy[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
    printf 'No extras files found under %s; nothing to install.\n' "${SRC_BASE}" >&2
    exit 0
  fi

  # Validate ownership and file types before any writes
  current_user="$(id -un 2>/dev/null || printf '')"
  for src in "${files_to_copy[@]}"; do
    [ -f "$src" ] || { log_error "EXTRAS" "extras entry not a regular file: $src"; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }
    [ -L "$src" ] && { log_error "EXTRAS" "extras file is symlink: $src"; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }
    src_owner="$(_get_owner "$src")"
    [ -n "$src_owner" ] && [ "$src_owner" != "$current_user" ] && { log_error "EXTRAS" "wrong owner: $src"; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }
  done

  # Copy files atomically preserving relative layout under DEST_BASE
  installed_providers=()
  installed_libs=()
  installed_docs=()
  installed_security=()
  installed_tests=()

  for src in "${files_to_copy[@]}"; do
    rel="${src#${SRC_BASE}/}"
    dest="${DEST_BASE}/${rel}"
    destdir="$(dirname "$dest")"
    mkdir -p "$destdir" 2>/dev/null || { log_error "EXTRAS" "cannot create dest dir: $destdir"; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }

    if type atomic_write >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      cat "$src" | atomic_write "$dest" 10 || { log_error "EXTRAS" "Error copying $src"; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }
    else
      cp -f "$src" "$dest" 2>/dev/null || { log_error "EXTRAS" "Error copying $src"; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }
      chmod 600 "$dest" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi

    chmod 600 "$dest" 2>/dev/null || true

    case "$rel" in
      providers/*.sh) installed_providers+=("$(basename "$rel" .sh)") ;;
      lib/*) installed_libs+=("$(basename "$rel")") ;;
      docs/*) installed_docs+=("$(basename "$rel")") ;;
      security/*) installed_security+=("$(basename "$rel")") ;;
      test/*) installed_tests+=("$(basename "$rel")") ;;
    esac
  done

  printf 'groqbash: INFO: Extras installed under %s\n' "${DEST_BASE}" >&2

  if [ "${#installed_providers[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
    printf 'Installed providers: %s\n' "$(printf '%s ' "${installed_providers[@]}")" >&2
  fi
  if [ "${#installed_libs[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
    printf 'Installed libs: %s\n' "$(printf '%s ' "${installed_libs[@]}")" >&2
  fi
  if [ "${#installed_docs[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
    printf 'Installed docs: %s\n' "$(printf '%s ' "${installed_docs[@]}")" >&2
  fi
  if [ "${#installed_security[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
    printf 'Installed security scripts: %s\n' "$(printf '%s ' "${installed_security[@]}")" >&2
  fi
  if [ "${#installed_tests[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
    printf 'Installed test scripts: %s\n' "$(printf '%s ' "${installed_tests[@]}")" >&2
  fi

  # Syntax check for installed providers
  for p in "${installed_providers[@]}"; do
    PROVIDER_MODULE_PATH="${DEST_PROV}/${p}.sh"
    if ! bash -n "$PROVIDER_MODULE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then
      printf 'groqbash: WARN: provider %s has syntax errors; check %s\n' "$p" "$PROVIDER_MODULE_PATH" >&2
    fi
  done

  exit 0
fi

summary: Normalize boolean-like flags: top-level loop that converts various flag variables to canonical 0/1 using is_truthy helper.

for flag in DRY_RUN STREAM_MODE INSTALL_EXTRAS PROVIDER_INTERACTIVE LIST_MODELS LIST_PROVIDERS CHAT_MODE QUIET SHOW_CONFIG DIAGNOSTICS; do

done

summary: Rispetta la selezione provider persistita a meno che non sia sovrascritta via CLI; gestisce selezione PROVIDER_CLI e persistenza atomica della scelta.
raw_snippet:
if [ -f "$(canonical_provider_file)" ] && [ -z "${PROVIDER_CLI:-}" ] && [ "${PROVIDER_INTERACTIVE:-0}" -eq 0 ]; then
  prov="$(sed -n '1p' "$(canonical_provider_file)" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  prov="$(printf '%s' "$prov" | awk '{$1=$1;print}')"
  [ -n "$prov" ] && PROVIDER="$prov"
fi

# CLI provider selection handling
if [ -n "${PROVIDER_CLI:-}" ] && [ "$PROVIDER_CLI" != "list" ]; then
  case " $SUPPORTED_PROVIDERS " in
    *" $PROVIDER_CLI "*) 
      prev_provider="$PROVIDER"
      PROVIDER="$PROVIDER_CLI"
      ;;
    *) log_error "PROVIDER" "unknown provider: $PROVIDER_CLI"; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP" ;;
  esac

  # Ensure config dir is available and writable
  if ! ensure_config_dir; then
    log_error "PROVIDER" "cannot persist provider selection: config dir unavailable."
    exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
  fi

  if [ -n "${prev_provider:-}" ] && [ "$prev_provider" != "$PROVIDER" ]; then
    if [ -n "${MODELS_FILE:-}" ] && [ -f "$MODELS_FILE" ]; then
      rm -f "$MODELS_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
  fi

  # Persist the explicit CLI-chosen provider (use PROVIDER_CLI to avoid race with other logic)
  target_provider="${PROVIDER_CLI}"
  target_provider_file="$(canonical_provider_file)"
  printf '%s\n' "$target_provider" | atomic_write "$target_provider_file" 10 \
    || { log_error "PROVIDER" "cannot persist provider selection to ${target_provider_file}."; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }
  chmod 600 "$target_provider_file" 2>/dev/null || true

fi

summary: Gestisce la selezione interattiva del provider, persiste la scelta, carica il modulo provider e gestisce il caso di esecuzione solo di configurazione.
raw_snippet:
if [ "$PROVIDER_INTERACTIVE" -eq 1 ] || [ "${PROVIDER_CLI:-}" = "list" ]; then
  IFS=' ' read -r -a _prov_arr <<EOF
$SUPPORTED_PROVIDERS
EOF

  current_default="$PROVIDER"
  if [ -f "$(canonical_provider_file)" ]; then
    pf="$(sed -n '1p' "$(canonical_provider_file)" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    pf="$(printf '%s' "$pf" | awk '{$1=$1;print}')"
    [ -n "$pf" ] && current_default="$pf"
  fi

  printf 'Select provider:\n' >&2
  i=1
  for p in "${_prov_arr[@]}"; do
    if [ "$p" = "$current_default" ]; then
      printf '%d) %s (default)\n' "$i" "$p" >&2
    else
      printf '%d) %s\n' "$i" "$p" >&2
    fi
    i=$((i+1))
  done

  printf '> ' >&2
  if ! IFS= read -r sel; then
    printf 'No selection made.\n' >&2
    exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
  fi
  sel="$(trim "$sel")"

  # If empty input, use current_default
  if [ -z "$sel" ]; then
    chosen="$current_default"
  else
    # If numeric selection, map to array index
    if printf '%s\n' "$sel" | grep -qE '^[0-9]+$'; then
      idx="$sel"
      chosen="${_prov_arr[$((idx-1))]}"
    else
      # treat input as provider name (case-sensitive match)
      chosen=""
      for p in "${_prov_arr[@]}"; do
        if [ "$p" = "$sel" ]; then
          chosen="$p"
          break
        fi
      done
    fi
  fi

  [ -n "$chosen" ] || { log_error "PROVIDER" "Selection out of range or unknown provider."; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }

  prev_provider="$PROVIDER"

  # Ensure config dir available
  if ! ensure_config_dir; then
    log_error "PROVIDER" "cannot persist provider selection: config dir unavailable."
    exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
  fi

  target_provider_file="$(canonical_provider_file)"
  if ! printf '%s\n' "$chosen" | atomic_write "$target_provider_file" 10; then
    log_error "PROVIDER" "cannot persist provider selection to $target_provider_file."
    exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
  fi
  chmod 600 "$target_provider_file" 2>/dev/null || true

  PROVIDER="$chosen"
  # Mark that provider was selected interactively in this invocation so we can
  # exit before prompt-processing if no positional args (configuration-only run).
  PROVIDER_INTERACTIVE_SELECTED=1

  if ! load_provider_module "$PROVIDER"; then
    log_error "PROVIDER" "failed to load provider module: $PROVIDER"
    exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
  fi

  if [ -n "${prev_provider:-}" ] && [ "$prev_provider" != "$PROVIDER" ]; then
    if [ -n "${MODELS_FILE:-}" ] && [ -f "$MODELS_FILE" ]; then
      rm -f "$MODELS_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
  fi

  # If this invocation was only to change provider (no positional args), exit now.
  if [ -n "${PROVIDER_INTERACTIVE_SELECTED:-}" ] && [ "$#" -eq 0 ]; then
    printf 'groqbash: INFO: Provider set to: %s\n' "${PROVIDER:-}" >&2
    exit 0
  fi
fi

summary: Crea provider-url file con default per 'groq' se mancante; carica il modulo provider finale e risolve l'URL del provider; definisce validate_provider_interface e verifica l'interfaccia del modulo.
raw_snippet:
# --- Minimal: create provider-url file with embedded default on first provider selection ---
# Only create provider-url if missing and provider is groq (best-effort, non-secret).
if [ "${PROVIDER:-}" = "groq" ]; then
  prov_file="$(canonical_provider_url_file)"
  if [ ! -f "$prov_file" ] || [ ! -s "$prov_file" ]; then
    # Use embedded default endpoint for groq
    embedded_url="https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/chat/completions"
    write_provider_url_if_missing "groq" "$embedded_url" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    # do not fail if write fails; it's best-effort
  fi
fi

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Load provider module for the chosen provider (ensure CLI/persisted cases are handled)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Call load_provider_module once for the final PROVIDER value. The function is
# guarded against double-loading (LOADED_PROVIDER_NAME), so this is safe even
# if the interactive branch already loaded the module.
if ! load_provider_module "$PROVIDER"; then
  log_error "PROVIDER" "failed to load provider module: $PROVIDER"
  exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
fi

# --- Minimal: resolve provider URL now (ENV > provider-url file > embedded default) ---
resolve_provider_url "$PROVIDER" || true
[ "${DEBUG:-0}" -eq 1 ] && printf 'DEBUG: provider=%s provider_url=%s\n' "$PROVIDER" "${GROQBASH_PROVIDER_URL:-<unset>}" >&2

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Validate provider module and interface (must run after provider module load)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Validate provider interface
validate_provider_interface() {
  local p="$1"
  local missing=0
  local required=( "buildpayload_${p}" "call_api_${p}" )
  local optional=( "call_api_streaming_${p}" "refresh_models_${p}" "validate_model_${p}" "auto_select_model_${p}" )
  local f

  for f in "${required[@]}"; do
    if ! type "$f" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      log_error "PROVIDER" "Provider '$p' module does not define required function $f()."
      missing=1
    fi
  done

  for f in "${optional[@]}"; do
    if ! type "$f" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      if [ "${DEBUG:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
        log_info "PROVIDER" "Provider '$p' missing optional function $f()"
      fi
    fi
  done

  return $missing
}

summary: Verifica che il modulo provider sia caricato e valido; gestisce REFRESH_MODELS e refresh dei modelli se MODELS_FILE mancante.
raw_snippet:
# Ensure provider module loaded and interface valid
if [ "${PROVIDER_MODULE_LOADED:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
  if ! validate_provider_interface "$PROVIDER"; then
    exit "$GROQBASHERRAPI"
  fi
else
  log_error "PROVIDER" "no valid provider loaded for '$PROVIDER'. Install or configure a provider plugin."
  exit "$GROQBASHERRAPI"
fi

# Handle explicit --refresh-models only after provider module is loaded and validated
if [ "${REFRESH_MODELS:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
  if ! ensure_api_key_for_provider "$PROVIDER"; then
    log_error "APIKEY" "API key required to refresh models for provider $PROVIDER."
    exit "$GROQBASHERRNOAPIKEY"
  fi
  refresh_models_dispatch "$MODELS_FILE" || exit "$GROQBASHERRAPI"
  exit 0
fi

# After provider module is loaded and validated, refresh models if local whitelist is missing
# This ensures refresh_models_dispatch invokes the provider-specific refresh implementation.
if [ ! -s "${MODELS_FILE:-}" ]; then
  if ! ensure_api_key_for_provider "$PROVIDER"; then
    log_error "APIKEY" "API key required to refresh models for provider $PROVIDER."
    # do not exit hard here; allow operation without refreshed models where possible
  else
    refresh_models_dispatch "$MODELS_FILE" || true
  fi
fi

summary: Definisce opzioni di stampa/config diagnostica e la funzione assemble_content che costruisce CONTENT da JSON_INPUT, file, template, stdin o ARGS preservando newline nelle sostituzioni.
raw_snippet:
#--2<---[ SECTION: CORE_PROVIDER_SHOW ]--->2--
# Show config / diagnostics
if [ "${PRINT_CONFIG_DIR:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
  printf '%s\n' "$(canonical_config_dir)"
  exit 0
fi

if [ "${PRINT_PROVIDER_FILE:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
  printf '%s\n' "$(canonical_provider_file)"
  exit 0
fi

if [ -n "${PRINT_MODEL_FILE:-0}" ]; then
  # PRINT_MODEL_FILE expected to contain provider name
  printf '%s\n' "$(canonical_model_file "${PRINT_MODEL_FILE}")"
  exit 0
fi

# Show full configuration (existing behavior), but use canonical helpers for model file lookup
if [ "${SHOW_CONFIG:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
  : "${ALLOWED_MODELS:=}"
  printf 'GroqBash configuration:\n'
  printf '  Version: %s (%s)\n' "$SCRIPT_VERSION" "$SCRIPT_DATE"
  printf '  Provider: %s\n' "$PROVIDER"
  printf '  Model (active): %s\n' "$MODEL"
  printf '  Model set via CLI: %s\n' "${MODEL_CLI_SET:-0}"

  # Determine model default file using persisted provider file if present
  if [ -f "$(canonical_provider_file)" ] && [ -s "$(canonical_provider_file)" ]; then
    cfg_prov="$(sed -n '1p' "$(canonical_provider_file)" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    cfg_prov="$(printf '%s' "$cfg_prov" | awk '{$1=$1;print}')"
    model_provider="${cfg_prov:-${PROVIDER:-groq}}"
  else
    model_provider="${PROVIDER:-groq}"
  fi

  MODEL_PROVIDER_CFG="$(canonical_model_file "${model_provider}")"
  if [ -s "$MODEL_PROVIDER_CFG" ]; then
    printf '  Model (default): %s\n' "$(sed -n '1p' "$MODEL_PROVIDER_CFG")"
  else
    printf '  Model (default): <none>\n'
  fi

  printf '  Config dir: %s\n' "$(canonical_config_dir)"
  printf '  Models dir: %s\n' "$GROQBASH_MODELS_DIR"
  printf '  Tmp dir: %s\n' "$GROQBASH_TMPDIR"
  printf '  History dir: %s\n' "$GROQBASH_HISTORY_DIR"
  printf '  Quiet: %s\n' "$QUIET"
  printf '  Debug: %s\n' "$DEBUG"
  printf '  Dry-run: %s\n' "$DRY_RUN"
  printf '  Whitelist models: %s\n' "$(printf '%s\n' "$ALLOWED_MODELS" | wc -l)"
  exit 0
fi

if [ "${DIAGNOSTICS:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
  : "${ALLOWED_MODELS:=}"
  printf 'GroqBash diagnostics:\n'

  printf '\n[Directories]\n'
  for d in "$GROQBASH_CONFIG_DIR" "$GROQBASH_MODELS_DIR" "$GROQBASH_TMPDIR" "$GROQBASH_EXTRAS_DIR" "$GROQBASH_HISTORY_DIR"; do
    if [ -d "$d" ]; then
      printf '  OK: %s\n' "$d"
    else
      printf '  ERROR: missing directory %s\n' "$d"
    fi
  done

  printf '\n[Provider]\n'
  printf '  Active provider: %s\n' "$PROVIDER"
  if type "buildpayload_${PROVIDER}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    printf '  Provider functions: OK\n'
  else
    printf '  ERROR: provider functions missing\n'
  fi

  printf '\n[Model]\n'
  printf '  Active model: %s\n' "$MODEL"
  if printf '%s\n' "$ALLOWED_MODELS" | grep -x -F -q "$MODEL"; then
    printf '  Model in whitelist: OK\n'
  else
    printf '  WARNING: model not in whitelist\n'
  fi

  printf '\n[API Key]\n'
  prov_upper="$(printf '%s' "$PROVIDER" | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]' | tr -c 'A-Z0-9_' '_')"
  check_var="${prov_upper}_API_KEY"
  custom_env_var_var="PROVIDER_API_ENV_${PROVIDER}"
  custom_env_var="${!custom_env_var_var:-}"
  if [ -n "$custom_env_var" ]; then check_var="$custom_env_var"; fi

  if [ -n "${!check_var:-}" ]; then
    printf '  API key: present (env %s)\n' "$check_var"
  else
    printf '  WARNING: API key missing (expected env %s)\n' "$check_var"
  fi

  printf '\n[Tmpdir]\n'
  if [ -d "$GROQBASH_TMPDIR" ]; then
    printf '  Tmpdir exists: OK\n'
  else
    printf '  ERROR: tmpdir missing\n'
  fi

  printf '\nDiagnostics complete.\n'
  exit 0
fi
#--2<---[ /SECTION: CORE_PROVIDER_SHOW ]--->2--

summary: Flusso principale: bootstrap opzionale, caricamento config locali, creazione RUN_TMPDIR, risoluzione e validazione del modello finale, lettura STDIN e definizione di assemble_content (continuazione).
raw_snippet:
#--3<---[ SECTION: CORE_PROVIDER_MAIN ]--->3--
# Main flow: assemble content, select model, and execute request(s)

# If requested, perform only structural bootstrap (PRECORE_BOOT + PRECORE_RUN + provider defs)
# and exit before any runtime side-effect (config load, model resolution, API, sessions, history).
if [ "${BOOTSTRAP_ONLY:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
  exit 0
fi

load_local_config
load_whitelist

# Ensure run tmpdir is available before proceeding with model selection and payload assembly
if ! ensure_run_tmpdir; then
  log_error "TMP" "cannot create or access RUN_TMPDIR ($GROQBASH_TMPDIR). Aborting."
  exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
fi

# Determine final model using centralized resolver
if ! resolve_model; then
  log_error "MODEL" "no model could be selected."
  exit "$GROQBASHERRBAD_MODEL"
fi

# FINAL_MODEL set by resolve_model()
# Validate core and provider-specific constraints
if ! validate_model_core "$FINAL_MODEL"; then
  log_error "MODEL" "model failed core validation: $FINAL_MODEL"
  exit "$GROQBASHERRBAD_MODEL"
fi

if ! validate_model_dispatch "$FINAL_MODEL"; then
  log_error "MODEL" "invalid model for provider $PROVIDER: $FINAL_MODEL"
  exit "$GROQBASHERRBAD_MODEL"
fi

MODEL="$FINAL_MODEL"

# Read stdin only when JSON_INPUT is not explicitly requested.
STDIN_CONTENT=""
if [ -z "${JSON_INPUT:-}" ] && [ ! -t 0 ]; then
  STDIN_CONTENT="$(cat - 2>/dev/null || true)"
fi

assemble_content() {
  CONTENT="${CONTENT:-}"
  local tmpl tmp_tmpl tmp_final extra file_content

  if [ -n "$JSON_INPUT" ]; then CONTENT=""; return 0; fi

  if [ "${#FILE_INPUTS[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
    CONTENT="$(collect_input_from_files "${FILE_INPUTS[@]}")"
    if [ "${#ARGS[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
      extra="$(expand_args_to_content)"
      [ -n "$extra" ] && CONTENT="${CONTENT}"$'\n\n'"$extra"
    fi
    return 0
  fi

  if [ -n "$TEMPLATE" ]; then
    if [ "${#FILE_INPUTS[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
      CONTENT="$(collect_input_from_files "${FILE_INPUTS[@]}")"
    elif [ -n "$STDIN_CONTENT" ]; then
      CONTENT="$STDIN_CONTENT"
    else
      if [ "${#ARGS[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then CONTENT="$(expand_args_to_content)"; else CONTENT=""; fi
    fi

    tmpl="$(cat "$GROQBASH_TEMPLATES_DIR/$TEMPLATE" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    ensure_run_tmpdir
    tmp_tmpl="$(_mktemp_in_dir "$RUN_TMPDIR" 2>/dev/null || true)"

    if [ -n "$tmp_tmpl" ]; then
      # Preserve original newlines while replacing the placeholder
      printf '%s' "$tmpl" | awk -v repl="$CONTENT" '{ gsub(/\{\{CONTENT\}\}/, repl); print }' > "$tmp_tmpl"
      tmp_final="${tmp_tmpl}.final"
      mv "$tmp_tmpl" "$tmp_final" 2>/dev/null || true
      file_content="$(cat "$tmp_final" 2>/dev/null || printf '%s' "$tmpl")"
      CONTENT="$file_content"
      rm -f "$tmp_final" 2>/dev/null || true
    else
      # Fallback: perform replacement while preserving newlines
      CONTENT="$(printf '%s' "$tmpl" | awk -v repl="$CONTENT" '{ gsub(/\{\{CONTENT\}\}/, repl); print }')"
    fi
    return 0
  fi

  if [ -n "$STDIN_CONTENT" ]; then
    CONTENT="$STDIN_CONTENT"
    if [ "${#ARGS[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
      extra="$(expand_args_to_content)"
      [ -n "$extra" ] && CONTENT="${CONTENT}"$'\n\n'"$extra"
    fi
    return 0
  fi

  if [ "${#ARGS[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then CONTENT="$(expand_args_to_content)"; else CONTENT=""; fi
  return 0
}

assemble_content

summary: Gestisce i controlli finali sull'input, batch processing, modalità chat interattiva, streaming e percorso non-streaming per l'esecuzione delle richieste e l'append alle sessioni.
raw_snippet:
# If provider was selected interactively in this invocation and there are no
# positional args / no content, treat this run as configuration-only and exit.
if [ -n "${PROVIDER_INTERACTIVE_SELECTED:-}" ] && [ "$#" -eq 0 ] && [ -z "${CONTENT:-}" ]; then
  printf 'groqbash: INFO: Provider set to: %s\n' "${PROVIDER:-}" >&2
  exit 0
fi

# Defensive check: ensure we actually have something to send as prompt/content.
# CONTENT may be set by assemble_content (from ARGS, files, stdin, template, JSON).
if [ -n "${JSON_INPUT:-}" ]; then
  : # JSON input present, proceed
elif [ -n "${CONTENT:-}" ]; then
  : # content assembled, proceed
elif [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; then
  # Positional args still present (fallback): join them into CONTENT
  CONTENT="$(printf '%s ' "$@")"
  CONTENT="${CONTENT%" "}" # trim trailing space
else
  # No prompt/content available: emit clear error and exit with input error code.
  log_error "INPUT" "no prompt provided."
  exit "$GROQBASHERRNO_PROMPT"
fi

if [ -z "$CONTENT" ] && [ -z "$JSON_INPUT" ] && [ "$CHAT_MODE" -eq 0 ] && [ -z "$BATCH_FILE" ]; then
  log_error "INPUT" "no prompt provided."
  exit "$GROQBASHERRNO_PROMPT"
fi

is_number "$TURE" || { log_error "ARGS" "--ture must be numeric"; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }
is_number "$MAX_TOKENS" || { log_error "ARGS" "--max must be numeric"; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }

# Final guard: if ALLOWED_MODELS is present, compare normalized model names
if [ -n "$ALLOWED_MODELS" ] && [ -n "$MODEL" ]; then
  norm_model="$(printf '%s' "$MODEL" | sed -e 's#^models/##' -e 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//')"
  if ! printf '%s\n' "$ALLOWED_MODELS" | grep -x -F -q "$norm_model"; then
    log_error "MODEL" "model '$MODEL' not supported or deprecated."
    printf '%s\n' "$ALLOWED_MODELS" >&2
    exit "$GROQBASHERRBAD_MODEL"
  fi
fi

ensure_run_tmpdir

# Batch processing
if [ -n "$BATCH_FILE" ]; then
  while IFS= read -r line || [ -n "$line" ]; do
    case "$line" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
    CONTENT="$line"

    # If sessioning is requested, validate and prepare messages file for buildpayload
    if [ -n "${SESSION_ID:-}" ]; then
      if ! session_validate_id "$SESSION_ID"; then
        log_error "SESSION" "invalid session id: $SESSION_ID"
        exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
      fi
      # Ensure sessions dir exists with strict perms
      mkdir -p "$GROQBASH_HISTORY_DIR/sessions" 2>/dev/null || true
      chmod 700 "$GROQBASH_HISTORY_DIR/sessions" 2>/dev/null || true

      # Enforce recommended window limit and warn if exceeded
      if [ -n "${SESSION_WINDOW:-}" ]; then
        if ! printf '%s' "$SESSION_WINDOW" | grep -qE '^[0-9]+$'; then
          SESSION_WINDOW=10
        fi
      else
        SESSION_WINDOW=10
      fi
      if [ "$SESSION_WINDOW" -gt 20 ]; then
        log_warn "SESSION" "session-window > 20 requested ($SESSION_WINDOW); recommended <= 20"
      fi

      # Prepare messages JSON file under RUN_TMPDIR and populate it (atomic)
      ensure_run_tmpdir || { log_error "SESSION" "cannot create run tmpdir"; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }
      BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE="${RUN_TMPDIR%/}/session-${SESSION_ID}-messages.json"
      export BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE

      # Try Session Engine first (Option A semantics: engine will honor --session-window override)
      if [ "${SE_AVAILABLE:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
        if ! session_engine_build_window "$SESSION_ID" "$SESSION_WINDOW" "${GROQBASH_SESSION_TARGET_BYTES:-}" "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
          # Engine failed: fallback to legacy session_read_window
          log_warn "SESSION" "session_engine_build_window failed; falling back to legacy session_read_window"
          session_read_window "$SESSION_ID" "$SESSION_WINDOW" "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" 2>/dev/null || log_warn "SESSION" "legacy session_read_window also failed"
        fi
      else
        # Engine not available: use legacy behavior
        session_read_window "$SESSION_ID" "$SESSION_WINDOW" "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" 2>/dev/null || log_warn "SESSION" "legacy session_read_window failed"
      fi

      # If BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE is still missing or invalid, ensure at least current user message is present
      if [ ! -f "${BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE:-}" ] || [ "$(jq -r '.messages | length' "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)" -eq 0 ]; then
        jq -n --arg role "user" --arg content "${CONTENT:-}" '{messages:[{role:$role,content:$content}] }' > "${BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE}"
      fi

      # Final fallback: ensure at least the current user message is present so API never receives messages:[]
      if [ ! -f "${BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE:-}" ] || [ "$(jq -r '.messages | length' "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)" -eq 0 ]; then
        jq -n --arg role "user" --arg content "${CONTENT:-}" '{messages:[{role:$role,content:$content}] }' > "${BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE}"
      fi
    fi

    # Now build payload (provider will honor BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE if present)
    build_payload_from_vars

    if ! ensure_api_key_for_provider "$PROVIDER"; then
      log_error "APIKEY" "missing API key; aborting batch item."
      continue
    fi

    if [ "$STREAM_MODE" -eq 1 ]; then
      call_api_streaming || log_error "CALL" "Error during streaming call."
    else
      perform_request_once || true
    fi

    printf '\n----\n'
  done < "$BATCH_FILE"
  exit 0
fi

# Interactive chat mode
if [ "$CHAT_MODE" -eq 1 ]; then
  if [ ! -t 0 ]; then log_error "CHAT" "interactive chat requires a TTY."; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; fi
  [ -t 1 ] && [ "$QUIET" -ne 1 ] && printf 'Entering interactive chat. Press Ctrl+D to exit.\n' >&2
  CONVERSATION=""
  while true; do
    if is_tty_out && [ "$QUIET" -ne 1 ] && { [ "$OUTPUT_MODE" = "text" ] || [ "$OUTPUT_MODE" = "raw" ]; }; then printf '\nYou: ' >&2; fi
    if ! IFS= read -r userline; then printf '\n' >&2; break; fi
    [ -z "$userline" ] && continue
    [ -z "$CONVERSATION" ] && CONVERSATION="$userline" || CONVERSATION="${CONVERSATION}"$'\n\n'"User: ${userline}"
    CONTENT="$userline"

    # Pre-JSON session hook: prepare BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE if sessioning requested
    if [ -n "${SESSION_ID:-}" ]; then
      if ! session_validate_id "$SESSION_ID"; then
        log_error "SESSION" "invalid session id: $SESSION_ID"
        exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
      fi
      mkdir -p "$GROQBASH_HISTORY_DIR/sessions" 2>/dev/null || true
      chmod 700 "$GROQBASH_HISTORY_DIR/sessions" 2>/dev/null || true

      if [ -n "${SESSION_WINDOW:-}" ]; then
        if ! printf '%s' "$SESSION_WINDOW" | grep -qE '^[0-9]+$'; then
          SESSION_WINDOW=10
        fi
      else
        SESSION_WINDOW=10
      fi
      if [ "$SESSION_WINDOW" -gt 20 ]; then
        log_warn "SESSION" "session-window > 20 requested ($SESSION_WINDOW); recommended <= 20"
      fi

      ensure_run_tmpdir || { log_error "SESSION" "cannot create run tmpdir"; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }
      BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE="$RUN_TMPDIR/session-${SESSION_ID}-messages.json"

      # Try Session Engine first; fallback to legacy on any failure
      if [ "${SE_AVAILABLE:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
        if ! session_engine_build_window "$SESSION_ID" "$SESSION_WINDOW" "${GROQBASH_SESSION_TARGET_BYTES:-}" "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
          log_warn "SESSION" "session_engine_build_window failed; falling back to legacy session_read_window"
          session_read_window "$SESSION_ID" "$SESSION_WINDOW" "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" || log_warn "SESSION" "legacy session_read_window failed"
        fi
      else
        session_read_window "$SESSION_ID" "$SESSION_WINDOW" "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" || log_warn "SESSION" "legacy session_read_window failed"
      fi

      export BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE
    fi

    build_payload_from_vars
    if ! ensure_api_key_for_provider "$PROVIDER"; then log_error "APIKEY" "missing API key; aborting chat turn."; continue; fi
    if [ "$STREAM_MODE" -eq 1 ]; then call_api_streaming || log_error "CALL" "Streaming call failed."
    else perform_request_once || log_error "CALL" "Call failed."; fi
  done
  exit 0
fi

# Single request (streaming or non-streaming)
if [ "$STREAM_MODE" -eq 1 ]; then

  # Pre-JSON session hook: prepare BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE if sessioning requested
  if [ -n "${SESSION_ID:-}" ]; then
    if ! session_validate_id "$SESSION_ID"; then
      log_error "SESSION" "invalid session id: $SESSION_ID"
      exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
    fi
    mkdir -p "$GROQBASH_HISTORY_DIR/sessions" 2>/dev/null || true
    chmod 700 "$GROQBASH_HISTORY_DIR/sessions" 2>/dev/null || true

    if [ -n "${SESSION_WINDOW:-}" ]; then
      if ! printf '%s' "$SESSION_WINDOW" | grep -qE '^[0-9]+$'; then
        SESSION_WINDOW=10
      fi
    else
      SESSION_WINDOW=10
    fi
    if [ "$SESSION_WINDOW" -gt 20 ]; then
      log_warn "SESSION" "session-window > 20 requested ($SESSION_WINDOW); recommended <= 20"
    fi

    ensure_run_tmpdir || { log_error "SESSION" "cannot create run tmpdir"; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }
    BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE="$RUN_TMPDIR/session-${SESSION_ID}-messages.json"

    # Try Session Engine first; fallback to legacy on any failure
    if [ "${SE_AVAILABLE:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
      if ! session_engine_build_window "$SESSION_ID" "$SESSION_WINDOW" "${GROQBASH_SESSION_TARGET_BYTES:-}" "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_warn "SESSION" "session_engine_build_window failed; falling back to legacy session_read_window"
        session_read_window "$SESSION_ID" "$SESSION_WINDOW" "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" || { log_warn "SESSION" "failed to read session window for $SESSION_ID"; rm -f "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true; }
      fi
    else
      session_read_window "$SESSION_ID" "$SESSION_WINDOW" "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" || { log_warn "SESSION" "failed to read session window for $SESSION_ID"; rm -f "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true; }
    fi

    export BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE
  fi

  build_payload_from_vars

  # Skip API key prompt in dry-run mode
  if [ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
    if [ "${DEBUG:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
      log_info "DRY" "dry-run: skipping API key check for provider $PROVIDER"
    fi
  else
    if ! ensure_api_key_for_provider "$PROVIDER"; then
      log_error "APIKEY" "missing API key; aborting."
      exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
    fi
  fi

  if call_api_streaming; then
     if [ "${DEBUG:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
       log_info "STREAM" "Streaming completed; captured response in $RESP"
     fi

     # If sessioning requested, append both user and assistant
     if [ -n "${SESSION_ID:-}" ]; then
      # 1) Append user message (preserve existing semantics) only if non-empty
      if [ -n "${CONTENT:-}" ]; then
        meta_source="cli"
        meta_cmd="$(session_sanitize_cmd "$0 ${ARGS[*]:-}")"
        meta_json="$(jq -c -n --arg source "$meta_source" --arg cmd "$meta_cmd" --arg id "" '{source:$source, cmd:$cmd, id:$id}')"

        if [ "${SE_AVAILABLE:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
          if ! session_engine_append "$SESSION_ID" "user" "$CONTENT" "$meta_json"; then
            log_warn "SESSION" "session_engine_append (user) failed; falling back to legacy session_append"
            session_append "$SESSION_ID" "user" "$CONTENT" "$meta_json" || log_warn "SESSION" "failed to append message to session $SESSION_ID (streaming)"
          fi
        else
          session_append "$SESSION_ID" "user" "$CONTENT" "$meta_json" || log_warn "SESSION" "failed to append message to session $SESSION_ID (streaming)"
        fi
      fi

      # 2) Append assistant response reconstructed from RESP (if present)
      # Only append assistant when not in DRY_RUN and when RESP contains data
      if [ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" -ne 1 ] && [ -s "${RESP:-}" ]; then
        # extract_text_from_resp uses the same extraction logic used by finalize_and_output
        assistant_text="$(extract_text_from_resp || true)"
        # Normalize whitespace-only responses away
        assistant_text="$(printf '%s' "$assistant_text" | sed -e 's/\r$//' -e '/^[[:space:]]*$/d' || true)"
        if [ -n "${assistant_text:-}" ]; then
          meta_source="provider"
          meta_json="$(jq -c -n --arg source "$meta_source" --arg model "$MODEL" --arg id "" '{source:$source, model:$model, id:$id}')"

          if [ "${SE_AVAILABLE:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
            if ! session_engine_append "$SESSION_ID" "assistant" "$assistant_text" "$meta_json"; then
              log_warn "SESSION" "session_engine_append (assistant) failed; falling back to legacy session_append"
              session_append "$SESSION_ID" "assistant" "$assistant_text" "$meta_json" || log_warn "SESSION" "failed to append assistant response to session $SESSION_ID (streaming)"
            fi
          else
            session_append "$SESSION_ID" "assistant" "$assistant_text" "$meta_json" || log_warn "SESSION" "failed to append assistant response to session $SESSION_ID (streaming)"
          fi
        fi
      fi
    fi

    printf '\n'
    exit 0
  else
    log_error "STREAM" "streaming request failed."
    exit "$GROQBASHERRCURL_FAILED"
  fi

else
  # Non-streaming single request path

  if is_tty_out && [ "$QUIET" -ne 1 ] && { [ "$OUTPUT_MODE" = "text" ] || [ "$OUTPUT_MODE" = "raw" ]; }; then
    printf '%b\n' "\033[1;36mYou:\033[0m" >&2
    if [ "${#ARGS[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
      anyfile=0
      for a in "${ARGS[@]}"; do [ -r "$a" ] && [ -f "$a" ] && { anyfile=1; break; }; done
      [ "$anyfile" -eq 1 ] && printf '%s\n' "(using file contents)" >&2 || printf '%s\n' "$CONTENT" >&2
    else
      printf '%s\n' "$CONTENT" >&2
    fi
    printf '%b\n' "\033[1;32mProvider ($PROVIDER, model: $MODEL):\033[0m" >&2
  fi

  # Pre-JSON session hook: prepare BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE if sessioning requested
  if [ -n "${SESSION_ID:-}" ]; then
    if ! session_validate_id "$SESSION_ID"; then
      log_error "SESSION" "invalid session id: $SESSION_ID"
      exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
    fi
    mkdir -p "$GROQBASH_HISTORY_DIR/sessions" 2>/dev/null || true
    chmod 700 "$GROQBASH_HISTORY_DIR/sessions" 2>/dev/null || true

    if [ -n "${SESSION_WINDOW:-}" ]; then
      if ! printf '%s' "$SESSION_WINDOW" | grep -qE '^[0-9]+$'; then
        SESSION_WINDOW=10
      fi
    else
      SESSION_WINDOW=10
    fi
    if [ "$SESSION_WINDOW" -gt 20 ]; then
      log_warn "SESSION" "session-window > 20 requested ($SESSION_WINDOW); recommended <= 20"
    fi

    ensure_run_tmpdir || { log_error "SESSION" "cannot create run tmpdir"; exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"; }
    BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE="$RUN_TMPDIR/session-${SESSION_ID}-messages.json"

    # Try Session Engine first; fallback to legacy on any failure
    if [ "${SE_AVAILABLE:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
      if ! session_engine_build_window "$SESSION_ID" "$SESSION_WINDOW" "${GROQBASH_SESSION_TARGET_BYTES:-}" "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_warn "SESSION" "session_engine_build_window failed; falling back to legacy session_read_window"
        session_read_window "$SESSION_ID" "$SESSION_WINDOW" "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" || { log_warn "SESSION" "failed to read session window for $SESSION_ID"; rm -f "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true; }
      fi
    else
      session_read_window "$SESSION_ID" "$SESSION_WINDOW" "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" || { log_warn "SESSION" "failed to read session window for $SESSION_ID"; rm -f "$BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true; }
    fi

    export BUILD_MESSAGES_FILE
  fi

  build_payload_from_vars

  # Skip API key prompt in dry-run mode
  if [ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
    if [ "${DEBUG:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
      log_info "DRY" "dry-run: skipping API key check for provider $PROVIDER"
    fi
  else
    if ! ensure_api_key_for_provider "$PROVIDER"; then
      log_error "APIKEY" "missing API key; aborting."
      exit "$GROQBASHERRTMP"
    fi
  fi

  # Perform request (with retries). After final attempt, append to session if requested.
  if perform_request_once; then
    # On success, append to session if SESSION_ID present (MVP)
    if [ -n "${SESSION_ID:-}" ]; then
      # 1) Append the user message (existing behavior) only if non-empty
      if [ -n "${CONTENT:-}" ]; then
        meta_source="cli"
        meta_cmd="$(session_sanitize_cmd "$0 ${ARGS[*]:-}")"
        meta_json="$(jq -c -n --arg source "$meta_source" --arg cmd "$meta_cmd" --arg id "" '{source:$source, cmd:$cmd, id:$id}')"

        if [ "${SE_AVAILABLE:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
          if ! session_engine_append "$SESSION_ID" "user" "$CONTENT" "$meta_json"; then
            log_warn "SESSION" "session_engine_append (user) failed; falling back to legacy session_append"
            session_append "$SESSION_ID" "user" "$CONTENT" "$meta_json" || log_warn "SESSION" "failed to append message to session $SESSION_ID"
          fi
        else
          session_append "$SESSION_ID" "user" "$CONTENT" "$meta_json" || log_warn "SESSION" "failed to append message to session $SESSION_ID"
        fi
      fi

      # 2) Append the assistant response (new)
      # Only append assistant when not in DRY_RUN and when RESP contains data
      if [ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" -ne 1 ] && [ -s "${RESP:-}" ]; then
        assistant_text="$(extract_text_from_resp || true)"
        assistant_text="$(printf '%s' "$assistant_text" | sed -e 's/\r$//' -e '/^[[:space:]]*$/d' || true)"
        if [ -n "${assistant_text:-}" ]; then
          meta_source="provider"
          meta_json="$(jq -c -n --arg source "$meta_source" --arg model "$MODEL" --arg id "" '{source:$source, model:$model, id:$id}')"

          if [ "${SE_AVAILABLE:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
            if ! session_engine_append "$SESSION_ID" "assistant" "$assistant_text" "$meta_json"; then
              log_warn "SESSION" "session_engine_append (assistant) failed; falling back to legacy session_append"
              session_append "$SESSION_ID" "assistant" "$assistant_text" "$meta_json" || log_warn "SESSION" "failed to append assistant response to session $SESSION_ID"
            fi
          else
            session_append "$SESSION_ID" "assistant" "$assistant_text" "$meta_json" || log_warn "SESSION" "failed to append assistant response to session $SESSION_ID"
          fi
        fi
      fi
    fi
    exit 0
  else
    # On failure, still attempt append (record of attempted message) but do not block
    if [ -n "${SESSION_ID:-}" ]; then
      if [ -n "${CONTENT:-}" ]; then
        meta_source="cli"
        meta_cmd="$(session_sanitize_cmd "$0 ${ARGS[*]:-}")"
        meta_json="$(jq -c -n --arg source "$meta_source" --arg cmd "$meta_cmd" --arg id "" '{source:$source, cmd:$cmd, id:$id}')"

        if [ "${SE_AVAILABLE:-0}" -eq 1 ]; then
          if ! session_engine_append "$SESSION_ID" "user" "$CONTENT" "$meta_json"; then
            log_warn "SESSION" "session_engine_append (user) failed after failed request; falling back to legacy session_append"
            session_append "$SESSION_ID" "user" "$CONTENT" "$meta_json" || log_warn "SESSION" "failed to append message to session $SESSION_ID after failed request"
          fi
        else
          session_append "$SESSION_ID" "user" "$CONTENT" "$meta_json" || log_warn "SESSION" "failed to append message to session $SESSION_ID after failed request"
        fi
      fi
    fi
    exit "$GROQBASHERRAPI"
  fi
fi
